summary: The main claim is: TLDR: “Key Phenomena in AI Risk” is an 8-week-long, facilitated reading group.
The main claim is: Policymakers should robustly regulate AI systems capable of deception.
Risks of AI Deception --------------------- After our survey of deceptive AI systems, we turn to considering the risks associated with AI systems.
The main claim is: For example, the grokking shown in this plot above is not sudden.
Why should we expect results from studying grokking to transfer?
The main claim is: If we want more people in the world who understand AGI safety, we need a place where it's accepted and encouraged to ask about the basics.
This is a safe space for people to ask stupid questions, so be kind!
The main claim is: This is embarrassing for me, even though shard theory just got confirmed.
I've written a lot about shard theory over the last year.

top_comment.author: Mart_Korz
top_comment.text: I notice that human DNA has 46 shards. This looks to me like evidence against the orthogonality thesis and for human values being much easier to reach than I expected.
top_comment.claim: The comment's point: This looks to me like evidence against the orthogonality thesis and for human values being much easier to reach than I expected.

summary: The main claim is: As the title says, Meta trained 4 foundational models with 7B, 13B, 33B, and 65B parameters respectively, and is open sourcing them for research.
On downstream benchmarks, the models do comparably well with Chinchilla and PaLM and only a bit worse than Flan-PaLM-540B and code-davinci-002 / text-davinci-002 .
The main claim is: As far as we are aware, GPT-4 will use the same 50,257 tokens as its two most recent predecessors.
